turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
IMC – Advertising Plan

1st step A communication situation analysis

What are the organization’s mission and vision?

What are their major communications initiatives? Have they been successful? Why or why not?

What are your organization’s biggest strengths related to communications?

What are your biggest challenges?

What are the most urgent communications needs for your organization?

Brand Imperatives What are the important features?

What’s the point of competitive advantage?

What’s the brand’s position relative to the competition? Also, what’s the brand essence, personality, and/or image? “What is the unique personality of the brand?

Tools to use company background, PEST-C, SWOT leading to the identification of the problem

2nd Step Communication Objectives

Put your Communication Objectives

What do we want customers to do in response to our messages? (perception, knowledge, feelings, symbolic meanings, attitudes and conviction, action)

Tools to use Facet Model

3rd step Target segment

Consumer Insights
What motivates the target? What are the “major truths” about the target’s relationship to the product category or brand?

Taking into consideration Key consumer and brand relationship insights, Message direction & Strategic consistency

 

4th step creative brief & media plan

• The Proposition or Selling Idea What is the single thought that the communication will bring to life in a provocative way?

• Creative Direction How can we best stimulate the desired response? How can we best say it?

• Media Imperatives Where and when should we say it? Actual media plan in Egyptian market

For references, use:

https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/1025401/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%201.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/1025402/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%202.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/894552/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%203.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/894553/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%204.pdf

https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/915029/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%206.pdf

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
IMC – Advertising Plan

1st Step: Communication Situation Analysis

Organization's Mission and Vision:
Our organization aims to provide top-quality, innovative products that improve the daily lives of our customers. We envision being a world leader in our industry, known for our commitment to enhancing the well-being of our customers, environmental stewardship, and social responsibility.

Major Communications Initiatives:
Our recent communications initiatives have focused on promoting our latest product line, highlighting our sustainability efforts, and increasing engagement on social media channels. Although these initiatives have generated some positive consumer feedback and online engagement, overall success has been limited in terms of improving brand awareness and generating sales.

Strengths Related to Communications:
Our biggest strengths include our passionate and dedicated team, innovative product offerings, and commitment to sustainability.

Challenges:
Our most significant challenges are increased competition, high customer expectations, and limited resources to execute effective communication campaigns.

Urgent Communications Needs:
The most urgent communications needs are to enhance our brand positioning, build stronger connections with our target audience, and increase visibility in the market.

Brand Imperatives:
Important features of our brand include innovation, sustainability, and customer-centric approach.

Competitive Advantage:
Our competitive advantage lies in our innovative product offerings, commitment to sustainability, and excellent customer service.

Brand Position, Essence, and Personality:
We position ourselves as a forward-thinking, eco-friendly brand in the industry, with a unique personality centered around innovation and commitment to enhancing our customers' well-being.

Tools to Use: Company background, PEST-C analysis, SWOT analysis to identify the communications problems.

2nd Step: Communication Objectives

Objectives:
1. Increase brand awareness among our target audience.
2. Improve the target audience's knowledge of our products and their unique features.
3. Create an emotional connection with our customers by evoking feelings of trust and loyalty.
4. Communicate the symbolic meanings of our brand, specifically our commitment to sustainability and innovation.
5. Encourage our target audience to take action by purchasing our products or engaging with our brand on social media.

Tools to Use: Facet Model.

3rd Step: Target Segment

Consumer Insights:
The primary motivation for our target audience is the desire for innovative, sustainable products that improve their daily lives. Major truths about their relationship with our brand include the appreciation for our commitment to the environment and the quality of our products.

Key consumer and brand relationship insights, message direction, and strategic consistency are essential for our advertising plan.

4th Step: Creative Brief & Media Plan

Proposition or Selling Idea:
Our communication should bring to life the idea that our brand offers innovative, eco-friendly products that improve customers' lives while contributing to a sustainable future.

Creative Direction:
We can stimulate the desired response by showcasing our product offerings in a visually appealing, informative, and emotionally compelling manner. This could include a combination of testimonial ads, engaging social media content, and educational blog posts.

Media Imperatives:
We should focus on both online and offline channels to reach our target audience effectively. This includes placing ads on relevant websites, social media platforms, print publications, and billboards in strategic locations within the Egyptian market. Additionally, engaging in collaborations with social influencers and partnering with environmentally-conscious organizations can also help boost our brand recognition.

References:

https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/1025401/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%201.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/1025402/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%202.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/894552/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%203.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/894553/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%204.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/902460/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%205.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/915029/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%206.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/915030/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%207.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/915031/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture%208.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/919404/mod_resource/content/1/Script%20%20Media%20plan.pdf

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
IMC – Orange Egypt's Advertising Plan

1st Step: Communication Situation Analysis

Organization's Mission and Vision:
Orange Egypt aims to provide reliable telecommunication services while fostering a customer-centric approach and delivering innovative solutions. Their vision is to be the leading telecommunication provider in the Egyptian market, known for their exceptional customer service, quality, and innovation.

Major Communications Initiatives:
Orange Egypt's major communication initiatives have included promoting their various mobile plans, devices, and internet packages, along with their commitment to digital inclusion and community empowerment. Some initiatives have been successful, such as promoting their bundles and offers, while others have not generated significant results, such as their efforts in CSR activities.

Biggest Strengths Related to Communications:
Orange Egypt's biggest strengths include their international brand recognition, comprehensive service offerings, and strong network coverage.

Biggest Challenges:
Their most significant challenges include intense competition, economic fluctuations, and evolving consumer demands and preferences.

Urgent Communications Needs:
The most urgent communications needs are to differentiate their brand from competitors, build stronger connections with their target audience, and improve their CSR visibility.

Brand Imperatives:
Important features of the Orange Egypt brand include extensive service offerings, excellent network coverage, and corporate social responsibility initiatives.

Competitive Advantage:
Orange Egypt's competitive advantage lies in their international brand recognition, extensive network coverage, and innovative services.

Brand Position, Essence, and Personality:
Orange Egypt positions itself as a customer-centric, innovative, and socially responsible telecommunication provider. The brand essence revolves around connectivity, reliability, and innovation.

Tools to Use: Company background, PEST-C analysis, SWOT analysis to identify the communications problem.

2nd Step: Communication Objectives

Objectives:
1. Increase brand awareness among the target audience in the Egyptian market.
2. Differentiate Orange Egypt from its competitors by effectively conveying its unique strengths and service propositions.
3. Strengthen the emotional bond with the target audience by emphasizing Orange Egypt's CSR initiatives.
4. Encourage customers to subscribe to Orange Egypt's services and increase customer retention.

Tools to Use: Facet Model.

3rd Step: Target Segment

Consumer Insights:
The target audience is motivated by affordable, reliable, and innovative telecommunication services. A key truth about their relationship with Orange is their appreciation for the brand's strong network coverage and customer-centric approach.

Key consumer and brand relationship insights, message direction, and strategic consistency are essential for the advertising plan.

4th Step: Creative Brief & Media Plan

Proposition or Selling Idea:
The communication will bring to life the idea that Orange Egypt offers unparalleled connectivity, innovation, and social responsibility in the Egyptian telecommunications market.

Creative Direction:
To stimulate the desired response, advertising efforts should include compelling visuals and narratives that showcase the exclusive features of Orange Egypt's services, their community initiatives, and their dedication to customer satisfaction.

Media Imperatives:
Orange Egypt should utilize various media channels to reach the target audience effectively. This includes TV commercials, social media campaigns, outdoor advertising, and collaborations with influencers. A mix of image and video content that showcases Orange's propositions and community activities will resonate with the target audience and help create a strong brand presence within the Egyptian market.

References:

https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/1025401/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture 1.pdf
https://lmsweb.aast.edu/pluginfile.php/1025402/mod_resource/content/1/Lecture 2.pdf
